title: Process Management
summary: Covers processes and threads, process states (running/ready/waiting), multitasking (preemptive and non-preemptive), scheduling, and the real-time clock role.
content_points: A process is a running program instance with its own memory zones; threads share a process's memory.
States: running (on CPU), ready (waiting for CPU), waiting (waiting for I/O or system call).
Preemptive multitasking: OS can forcibly deactivate a process after its time slice (clock interrupt).
Non-preemptive: process runs until it voluntarily yields or makes a system call.
Real-time clock generates periodic interrupts to give OS control for scheduling.
Thread scheduling: by OS or by application via a threading library.
learning_objectives: Describe the states of a process and their transitions
Explain the role of the real-time clock in scheduling
